structures.name: IterableElementBase
structures.description:
Base class that makes a data structure iterable and provides common
element-wise utilities (e.g., map/filter/reduce/find).

methods.name: [Symbol.iterator]
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the structure's elements.

methods.name: values
methods.description: Returns an iterator over the values (alias of the default iterator).

methods.name: every
methods.description: Tests whether all elements satisfy the predicate.

methods.name: some
methods.description: Tests whether at least one element satisfies the predicate.

methods.name: forEach
methods.description: Invokes a callback for each element in iteration order.

methods.name: has
methods.description: Checks whether a strictly-equal element exists in the structure.

methods.name: reduce
methods.description: Reduces all elements to a single accumulated value.

methods.name: toArray
methods.description: Materializes the elements into a new array.

methods.name: toVisual
methods.description:
Returns a representation of the structure suitable for quick visualization.
Defaults to an array of elements; subclasses may override to provide richer visuals.

methods.name: print
methods.description: Prints `toVisual()` to the console. Intended for quick debugging.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Indicates whether the structure currently contains no elements.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Removes all elements from the structure.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Creates a structural copy with the same element values and configuration.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Maps each element to a new element and returns a new iterable structure.

methods.name: mapSame
methods.description: Maps each element to the same element type and returns the same concrete structure type.

methods.name: filter
methods.description: Filters elements using the provided predicate and returns the same concrete structure type.

structures.name: IterableEntryBase
structures.description: Iterable view over key-value entries.

methods.name: [Symbol.iterator]
methods.description: Default iterator yielding `[key, value]` entries.

methods.name: entries
methods.description: Iterate over `[key, value]` pairs (may yield `undefined` values).

methods.name: keys
methods.description: Iterate over keys only.

methods.name: values
methods.description: Iterate over values only.

methods.name: every
methods.description: Test whether all entries satisfy the predicate.

methods.name: some
methods.description: Test whether any entry satisfies the predicate.

methods.name: forEach
methods.description: Visit each entry, left-to-right.

methods.name: find
methods.description: Find the first entry that matches a predicate.

methods.name: has
methods.description: Whether the given key exists.

methods.name: hasValue
methods.description: Whether there exists an entry with the given value.

methods.name: get
methods.description: Get the value under a key.

methods.name: reduce
methods.description: Reduce entries into a single accumulator.

methods.name: toArray
methods.description: Converts data structure to `[key, value]` pairs.

methods.name: toVisual
methods.description: Visualize the iterable as an array of `[key, value]` pairs (or a custom string).

methods.name: print
methods.description: Print a human-friendly representation to the console.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Whether there are no entries.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all entries.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone preserving the concrete subtype.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Map entries using an implementation-specific strategy.

methods.name: filter
methods.description: Filter entries and return the same-species structure.

structures.name: LinkedListNode
structures.description: Singly-linked list node.

structures.name: LinearBase
structures.description: Abstract linear container with array-like utilities.

methods.name: indexOf
methods.description: First index of a value from the left.

methods.name: lastIndexOf
methods.description: Last index of a value from the right.

methods.name: findIndex
methods.description: Find the first index matching a predicate.

methods.name: concat
methods.description: Concatenate elements and/or containers.

methods.name: sort
methods.description: In-place stable order via array sort semantics.

methods.name: splice
methods.description: Remove and/or insert elements at a position (array-compatible).

methods.name: join
methods.description: Join all elements into a string.

methods.name: toReversedArray
methods.description: Snapshot elements into a reversed array.

methods.name: slice
methods.description: Create a shallow copy of a subrange.

methods.name: fill
methods.description: Fill a range with a value.

methods.name: setAt
methods.description: Set the value at an index.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone while preserving concrete subtype.

methods.name: reverse
methods.description: Reverse the order of elements in-place (or equivalent).

methods.name: push
methods.description: Append one element or node to the tail.

methods.name: pushMany
methods.description: Append many elements/nodes at once.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Remove one element or node if present.

methods.name: at
methods.description: Get element at an index.

methods.name: deleteAt
methods.description: Remove element at a position.

methods.name: addAt
methods.description: Insert an element/node at a position.

structures.name: LinearLinkedBase
structures.description: Linked-list specialized linear container.

methods.name: indexOf
methods.description: Linked-list optimized `indexOf` (forwards scan).

methods.name: lastIndexOf
methods.description: Linked-list optimized `lastIndexOf` (reverse scan).

methods.name: concat
methods.description: Concatenate lists/elements preserving order.

methods.name: slice
methods.description: Slice via forward iteration (no random access required).

methods.name: splice
methods.description: Splice by walking node iterators from the start index.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete by element or node in a linked list.

methods.name: addBefore
methods.description: Insert new element/node before an existing node.

methods.name: addAfter
methods.description: Insert new element/node after an existing node.

methods.name: getNodeAt
methods.description: Node at index (for random-access emulation).

structures.name: AVLTreeNode
structures.description:
Represents a Node in an AVL (Adelson-Velsky and Landis) Tree.
It extends a BSTNode and ensures the 'height' property is maintained.

structures.name: AVLTree
structures.description:
Represents a self-balancing AVL (Adelson-Velsky and Landis) Tree.
This tree extends BST and performs rotations on set/delete to maintain balance.

methods.name: createNode
methods.description: (Protected) Creates a new AVL tree node.

methods.name: isNode

parameters.name: keyNodeOrEntry
parameters.type: K | AVLTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined

methods.description: Checks if the given item is an `AVLTreeNode` instance.

methods.name: set

parameters.name: keyNodeOrEntry
parameters.type: K | AVLTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined

methods.description: Sets a new node to the AVL tree and balances the tree path.

methods.name: delete

parameters.name: keyNodeOrEntry
parameters.type: K | AVLTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined

methods.description: Deletes a node from the AVL tree and re-balances the tree.

methods.name: perfectlyBalance
methods.description: Rebuilds the tree to be perfectly balanced.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Creates a new AVLTree by mapping each [key, value] pair.

methods.name: readSingle
methods.description: The function "readSingle" reads a single number from a specified index.

methods.name: update
methods.description:
The "update" function updates the value at a given index by adding a delta and triggers a callback
to notify of the change.

methods.name: writeSingle
methods.description: The function "writeSingle" checks the index and writes a single value with a given frequency.

methods.name: read
methods.description:
The read function takes a count parameter, checks if it is an integer, and returns the result of
calling the _read function with the count parameter clamped between 0 and the maximum value.

methods.name: lowerBound
methods.description: The function returns the lower bound of a non-descending sequence that sums up to a given number.

methods.name: upperBound
methods.description:
The upperBound function returns the index of the first element in a sequence that is greater than
or equal to a given sum.

methods.name: getPrefixSum
methods.description: The function calculates the prefix sum of an array using a binary indexed tree.

structures.name: BinaryTree
structures.description: A general Binary Tree implementation.

Keys are ordered, allowing for faster search operations compared to a standard Binary Tree.

structures.name: RedBlackTree
structures.description: Represents a Red-Black Tree (self-balancing BST) supporting map-like mode and stable O(log n) updates.

methods.name: createNode
methods.description: Create a red-black node for the given key/value (value ignored in map mode).

methods.name: isNode

parameters.name: keyNodeOrEntry
parameters.type: K | RedBlackTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined

methods.description: Type guard: check whether the input is a RedBlackTreeNode.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all nodes and clear the key→value store (if in map mode).

methods.name: setWithHintNode
methods.description:
Insert/update using a hint node to speed up nearby insertions.

close to the expected insertion position (often the previously returned node in a loop).

When the hint is a good fit (sorted / nearly-sorted insertion), this can avoid most of the
normal root-to-leaf search and reduce constant factors.

When the hint does not match (random workloads), this will fall back to the normal set path.

methods.name: setWithHint
methods.description: Boolean wrapper for setWithHintNode.

methods.name: set

parameters.name: keyNodeOrEntry
parameters.type: K | RedBlackTreeNode<K, V> | [K | null | undefined, V | undefined] | null | undefined

methods.description:
Insert or update a key/value (map mode) or key-only (set mode).

This method is optimized for:
- monotonic inserts via min/max boundary fast paths
- updates via a single-pass search (no double walk)

methods.name: delete

parameters.name: keyNodeEntryRawOrPredicate
parameters.type: BTNRep<K, V, RedBlackTreeNode<K, V>> | NodePredicate<RedBlackTreeNode<K, V> | null>

methods.description: Delete a node by key/node/entry and rebalance as needed.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Transform entries into a like-kind red-black tree with possibly different key/value types.

methods.name: build
methods.description:
The build function creates a segment tree by recursively dividing the given range into smaller segments and assigning
the sum of values to each segment.

methods.name: updateNode
methods.description: The function updates the value of a node in a segment tree and recalculates the sum of its children if they exist.

methods.name: querySumByRange
methods.description: The function `querySumByRange` calculates the sum of values within a given range in a segment tree.

structures.name: TreeMap
structures.description:
An ordered Map backed by a red-black tree.

- Iteration order is ascending by key.
- No node exposure: all APIs use keys/values only.

methods.name: createDefaultComparator
methods.description:
Create the strict default comparator.

Supports:
- `number` (rejects `NaN`; treats `-0` and `0` as equal)
- `string`
- `Date` (orders by `getTime()`, rejects invalid dates)

For other key types, a custom comparator must be provided.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Whether the map is empty.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Set or overwrite a value for a key.

methods.name: get
methods.description: Get the value under a key.

methods.name: has
methods.description: Test whether a key exists.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete a key.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all entries.

methods.name: keys
methods.description: Iterate over keys in ascending order.

methods.name: values
methods.description:
Iterate over values in ascending key order.

Note: values may be `undefined` (TreeMap allows storing `undefined`, like native `Map`).

methods.name: entries
methods.description:
Iterate over `[key, value]` entries in ascending key order.

Note: values may be `undefined`.

methods.name: forEach

parameters.name: cb
parameters.type: (value: V | undefined, key: K, map: TreeMap<K, V, [K, V]>) => void

methods.description:
Visit each entry in ascending key order.

Note: callback value may be `undefined`.

methods.name: map

parameters.name: callbackfn
parameters.type: TreeMapEntryCallback<K, V, [MK, MV], TreeMap<K, V, [K, V]>>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: Omit<TreeMapOptions<MK, MV, [MK, MV]>, "toEntryFn"> & { comparator?: ((a: MK, b: MK) => number) | undefined; }

methods.description:
Create a new TreeMap by mapping each entry to a new `[key, value]` entry.

This mirrors `RedBlackTree.map`: mapping produces a new ordered container.

methods.name: filter

parameters.name: callbackfn
parameters.type: TreeMapEntryCallback<K, V, boolean, TreeMap<K, V, [K, V]>>

methods.description: Create a new TreeMap containing only entries that satisfy the predicate.

methods.name: reduce

parameters.name: callbackfn
parameters.type: TreeMapReduceCallback<K, V, A, TreeMap<K, V, [K, V]>>

methods.description: Reduce entries into a single accumulator.

structures.name: TreeMultiMapNode
structures.description: Node type used by TreeMultiMap (alias to RedBlackTreeNode for backward compatibility).

structures.name: TreeMultiMap
structures.description:
TreeMultiMap (ordered MultiMap) — key → bucket (Array of values).

Semantics (RFC):
- Bucketed design: each key appears once; duplicates live in the bucket.
- `get(key)` returns a **live** bucket reference.
- Default iteration yields bucket entries: `[K, V[]]`.
- Navigable operations (`first/last/ceiling/...`) return entry tuples like TreeMap.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Whether the map is empty.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Removes all entries from the map.

methods.name: count
methods.description: Bucket length for a key (missing => 0).

methods.name: has
methods.description: Whether the map contains the given key.

methods.name: get
methods.description: Live bucket reference (do not auto-delete key if bucket becomes empty via mutation).

methods.name: add
methods.description: Append a single value.

parameters.name: entry
parameters.type: K | [K | null | undefined, V[] | undefined] | null | undefined

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Deletes a key and its entire bucket.

methods.name: hasEntry
methods.description: Check if a specific value exists in a key's bucket.

methods.name: deleteValue
methods.description: Delete a single occurrence of a value from a key's bucket.

methods.name: deleteValues
methods.description: Delete all occurrences of a value from a key's bucket.

methods.name: [Symbol.iterator]
methods.description: Iterates over all entries as [key, bucket] pairs.

methods.name: keys
methods.description: Iterates over all keys.

methods.name: values
methods.description: Iterates over all buckets.

methods.name: entriesOf
methods.description: Iterates over all entries for a specific key.

methods.name: valuesOf
methods.description: Iterates over all values for a specific key.

methods.name: flatEntries
methods.description: Iterates over all [key, value] pairs (flattened from buckets).

methods.name: first
methods.description: Returns the entry with the smallest key.

methods.name: last
methods.description: Returns the entry with the largest key.

methods.name: pollFirst
methods.description: Removes and returns the entry with the smallest key.

methods.name: pollLast
methods.description: Removes and returns the entry with the largest key.

methods.name: ceiling
methods.description: Returns the entry with the smallest key >= given key.

methods.name: floor
methods.description: Returns the entry with the largest key <= given key.

methods.name: higher
methods.description: Returns the entry with the smallest key > given key.

methods.name: lower
methods.description: Returns the entry with the largest key < given key.

methods.name: print
methods.description: Prints the internal tree structure (for debugging).

methods.description: Executes a callback for each entry.

structures.name: TreeSet
structures.description:
An ordered Set backed by a red-black tree.

- Iteration order is ascending by key.
- No node exposure: all APIs use keys only.

methods.name: createDefaultComparator
methods.description:
Create the strict default comparator.

Supports:
- `number` (rejects `NaN`; treats `-0` and `0` as equal)
- `string`
- `Date` (orders by `getTime()`, rejects invalid dates)

For other key types, a custom comparator must be provided.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Whether the set is empty.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Add a key to the set (no-op if already present).

methods.name: has
methods.description: Test whether a key exists.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete a key.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all keys.

methods.name: keys
methods.description: Iterate over keys in ascending order.

methods.name: values
methods.description:
Iterate over values in ascending order.

Note: for Set-like containers, `values()` is the same as `keys()`.

methods.name: entries
methods.description:
Iterate over `[value, value]` pairs (native Set convention).

Note: TreeSet stores only keys internally; `[k, k]` is created on-the-fly during iteration.

methods.name: forEach

parameters.name: cb
parameters.type: (value: K, value2: K, set: TreeSet<K, K>) => void

methods.description:
Visit each value in ascending order.

Callback follows native Set convention: `(value, value2, set)`.

This mirrors `RedBlackTree.map`: mapping produces a new ordered container.

methods.description: The `get` function returns the value at the specified row and column index if it is a valid index.

methods.name: set
methods.description: The set function updates the value at a specified row and column in a two-dimensional array.

methods.name: isMatchForCalculate
methods.description:
The function checks if the dimensions of the given matrix match the dimensions of the current
matrix.

methods.name: add
methods.description: The `add` function adds two matrices together, returning a new matrix with the result.

methods.name: subtract
methods.description:
The `subtract` function performs element-wise subtraction between two matrices and returns a new
matrix with the result.

methods.name: multiply
methods.description:
The `multiply` function performs matrix multiplication between two matrices and returns the result
as a new matrix.

methods.name: transpose
methods.description:
The transpose function takes a matrix and returns a new matrix that is the transpose of the
original matrix.

methods.name: inverse
methods.description: The `inverse` function calculates the inverse of a square matrix using Gaussian elimination.

methods.name: dot
methods.description: The dot function calculates the dot product of two matrices and returns a new matrix.

methods.name: isValidIndex
methods.description: The function checks if a given row and column index is valid within a specified range.

methods.name: clone
methods.description:
The `clone` function returns a new instance of the Matrix class with the same data and properties
as the original instance.

methods.name: start
methods.description:
The "start" function moves the character in its current direction until it encounters an obstacle, then it turns the
character and repeats the process.

methods.name: check
methods.description: The function checks if there is a valid move in the specified direction in a matrix.

methods.name: move
methods.description: The `move` function updates the current position based on the given direction and updates the matrix accordingly.

structures.name: MaxPriorityQueue
structures.description:
Max-oriented priority queue (max-heap) built on {@link PriorityQueue}.
The default comparator orders primitive values in descending order. If you store objects,
you must provide a custom comparator via {@link PriorityQueueOptions}.

structures.name: MinPriorityQueue
structures.description:
Min-oriented priority queue (min-heap) built on {@link PriorityQueue}.
The queue removes the smallest element first under the provided comparator.
Provide a custom comparator if you store non-primitive objects.

structures.name: Deque
structures.description: Deque implemented with circular buckets allowing O(1) amortized push/pop at both ends.

methods.name: fromArray

parameters.name: this
parameters.type: new (elements?: IterableWithSizeOrLength<E> | IterableWithSizeOrLength<R> | undefined, options?: DequeOptions<E, R> | undefined) => any

methods.description: Create a Deque from an array of elements.

methods.name: push
methods.description: Append one element at the back.

methods.name: pop
methods.description: Remove and return the last element.

methods.name: shift
methods.description: Remove and return the first element.

methods.name: unshift
methods.description: Prepend one element at the front.

methods.name: pushMany
methods.description: Append a sequence of elements.

methods.name: unshiftMany
methods.description: Prepend a sequence of elements.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Check whether the deque is empty.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all elements and reset structure.

methods.name: at
methods.description: Get the element at a given position.

methods.name: setAt
methods.description: Replace the element at a given position.

methods.name: addAt
methods.description: Insert repeated copies of an element at a position.

methods.name: cut
methods.description: Cut the deque to keep items up to index; optionally mutate in-place.

methods.name: splice
methods.description: Remove and/or insert elements at a position (array-like behavior).

methods.name: cutRest
methods.description: Cut the deque to keep items from index onward; optionally mutate in-place.

methods.name: deleteAt
methods.description: Delete the element at a given position.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete the first occurrence of a value.

methods.name: deleteWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.type: (value: E, index: number, deque: this) => boolean

methods.description: Delete the first element matching a predicate.

methods.name: setEquality
methods.description: Set the equality comparator used by delete operations.

methods.name: reverse
methods.description: Reverse the deque by reversing buckets and pointers.

methods.name: unique
methods.description: Deduplicate consecutive equal elements in-place.

methods.name: shrinkToFit
methods.description: Trim unused buckets to fit exactly the active range.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone this deque, preserving options.

methods.name: filter
methods.description: Filter elements into a new deque of the same class.

methods.name: mapSame
methods.description: Map elements into a new deque of the same element type.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Map elements into a new deque (possibly different element type).

structures.name: Queue
structures.description: Array-backed queue with amortized O(1) enqueue/dequeue via an offset pointer and optional auto-compaction.

methods.name: fromArray
methods.description: Create a queue from an array of elements.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Check whether the queue is empty.

methods.name: push
methods.description: Enqueue one element at the back.

methods.name: pushMany
methods.description: Enqueue many elements from an iterable.

methods.name: shift
methods.description: Dequeue one element from the front (amortized via offset).

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete the first occurrence of a specific element.

methods.name: at
methods.description: Get the element at a given logical index.

methods.name: deleteAt
methods.description: Delete the element at a given index.

methods.name: addAt
methods.description: Insert a new element at a given index.

methods.name: setAt
methods.description: Replace the element at a given index.

methods.name: reverse
methods.description: Reverse the queue in-place by compacting then reversing.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all elements and reset offset.

methods.name: compact
methods.description: Compact storage by discarding consumed head elements.

methods.name: splice
methods.description: Remove and/or insert elements at a position (array-like).

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone this queue and its parameters.

methods.name: filter
methods.description: Filter elements into a new queue of the same class.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Map each element to a new element in a possibly different-typed queue.

methods.name: mapSame
methods.description: Map each element to a new value of the same type.

structures.name: LinkedListQueue
structures.description: Queue implemented over a singly linked list; preserves head/tail operations with linear scans for queries.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone this linked-list-based queue.

structures.name: Stack
structures.description: LIFO stack with array storage and optional record→element conversion.

methods.name: fromArray

parameters.name: this
parameters.type: new (elements?: Iterable<E> | Iterable<R> | undefined, options?: StackOptions<E, R> | undefined) => any

methods.description: Create a stack from an array of elements.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Check whether the stack is empty.

methods.name: peek
methods.description: Get the top element without removing it.

methods.name: push
methods.description: Push one element onto the top.

methods.name: pop
methods.description: Pop and return the top element.

methods.name: pushMany
methods.description: Push many elements from an iterable.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete the first occurrence of a specific element.

methods.name: deleteAt
methods.description: Delete the element at an index.

methods.name: deleteWhere

parameters.name: predicate
parameters.type: (value: E, index: number, stack: this) => boolean

methods.description: Delete the first element that satisfies a predicate.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all elements and reset storage.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone this stack.

methods.name: filter
methods.description: Filter elements into a new stack of the same class.

methods.name: mapSame
methods.description: Map values into a new stack of the same element type.

methods.name: map
methods.description: Map values into a new stack (possibly different element type).

methods.name: setEquality
methods.description: Set the equality comparator used by delete/search operations.

methods.name: addChildren
methods.description: The function `addChildren` adds one or more child nodes to the current node.

methods.name: getHeight
methods.description:
The function `getHeight()` calculates the maximum depth of a tree structure by performing a
breadth-first search.

structures.name: TrieNode
structures.description: Node used by Trie to store one character and its children.

structures.name: Trie
structures.description: Prefix tree (Trie) for fast prefix queries and word storage.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Insert one word into the trie.

methods.name: addMany
methods.description: Insert many words from an iterable.

methods.name: has
methods.description: Check whether a word exists.

methods.name: isEmpty
methods.description: Check whether the trie is empty.

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Remove all words and reset to a fresh root.

methods.name: delete
methods.description: Delete one word if present.

methods.name: getHeight
methods.description: Compute the height (max depth) of the trie.

methods.name: hasPurePrefix
methods.description: Check whether input is a proper prefix of at least one word.

methods.name: hasPrefix
methods.description: Check whether any word starts with input.

methods.name: hasCommonPrefix
methods.description: Check whether the trie’s longest common prefix equals input.

methods.name: getLongestCommonPrefix
methods.description: Return the longest common prefix among all words.

methods.name: getWords
methods.description: Collect words under a prefix up to a maximum count.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Deep clone this trie by iterating and inserting all words.

methods.name: filter
methods.description: Filter words into a new trie of the same class.

methods.name: mapSame
methods.description: Map words into a new trie of the same element type.
